udt_enum_v0.doc:
The state of a proposal in its lifecycle.

States are divided into two categories:

## Time-based states (derived, never stored explicitly)

These are computed by [`get_proposal_state()`] from the current ledger
relative to the proposal's voting schedule. They are only returned when
no explicit state has been set.

- [`Pending`](ProposalState::Pending) \xe2\x80\x94 voting has not started yet.
- [`Active`](ProposalState::Active) \xe2\x80\x94 voting is ongoing.
- [`Defeated`](ProposalState::Defeated) \xe2\x80\x94 voting ended **without** the
counting logic marking the proposal as `Succeeded`.

## Explicit states

Set explicitly by the Governor or its extensions and persisted in
storage. Once set, they take precedence over any time-based derivation.

- [`Canceled`](ProposalState::Canceled) \xe2\x80\x94 set by the Governor.
- [`Succeeded`](ProposalState::Succeeded) \xe2\x80\x94 set by the counting logic.
- [`Queued`](ProposalState::Queued) / [`Expired`](ProposalState::Expired) \xe2\x80\x94
set by extensions like `TimelockControl`.
- [`Executed`](ProposalState::Execu
udt_enum_v0.name: ProposalState

cases.doc: The proposal is pending and voting has not started yet.
cases.name: Pending

cases.doc: The proposal is active and voting is ongoing.
cases.name: Active

cases.doc: The proposal was defeated (did not meet quorum or majority). This is\nthe default outcome when voting ends and the counting logic has\nnot marked the proposal as [`Succeeded`](ProposalState::Succeeded).
cases.name: Defeated

cases.doc: The proposal has been cancelled. Set by the Governor.
cases.name: Canceled

cases.doc:
The proposal succeeded and can be executed. Set by the counting
logic when the proposal meets the required quorum and vote
thresholds. If a queuing extension is enabled, this state means the
proposal is ready to be queued.
cases.name: Succeeded

cases.doc: The proposal is queued for execution. Set by extensions like\n`TimelockControl`.
cases.name: Queued

cases.doc: The proposal has expired and can no longer be executed. Set by\nextensions like `TimelockControl`.
cases.name: Expired

cases.doc: The proposal has been executed. Set by the Governor.
cases.name: Executed

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Represents a operation to be executed by the timelock.

An operation encapsulates all the information needed to invoke a function
on a target contract after the timelock delay has passed.
udt_struct_v0.name: Operation

fields.doc: The serialized arguments to pass to the function
fields.name: args

fields.doc: The function name to invoke on the target contract
fields.name: function

fields.doc: Hash of a predecessor operation that must be executed first.\nUse BytesN::<32>::from_array(&[0u8; 32]) for no predecessor.
fields.name: predecessor

fields.doc: A salt value for operation uniqueness.\nAllows scheduling the same operation multiple times with different IDs.
fields.name: salt

fields.doc: The contract address to call
fields.name: target

udt_union_v0.doc:
Hook types for modular compliance system.

Each hook type represents a specific event or validation point
where compliance modules can be executed.
udt_union_v0.name: ComplianceHook

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully transferred from one wallet to\nanother. Modules registered for this hook can update their state\nbased on transfer events.
void_v0.name: Transferred

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully created/minted to a wallet.\nModules registered for this hook can update their state based on minting\nevents.
void_v0.name: Created

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully destroyed/burned from a wallet.\nModules registered for this hook can update their state based on burning\nevents.
void_v0.name: Destroyed

void_v0.doc:
Called during transfer validation to check if a transfer should be
allowed. Modules registered for this hook can implement transfer
restrictions. This is a READ-only operation and should not modify
state.
void_v0.name: CanTransfer

void_v0.doc:
Called during mint validation to check if a mint operation should be
allowed. Modules registered for this hook can implement transfer
restrictions. This is a READ-only operation and should not modify
state.
void_v0.name: CanCreate

cases.doc: Indicates a module is already registered for this hook.
cases.name: ModuleAlreadyRegistered

cases.doc: Indicates a module is not registered for this hook.
cases.name: ModuleNotRegistered

cases.doc: Indicates a module bound is exceeded.
cases.name: ModuleBoundExceeded

cases.doc: Indicates a token is not bound to this compliance contract.
cases.name: TokenNotBound

cases.name: CountryDataNotFound

cases.doc: Identity can't be with empty country data list.
cases.name: EmptyCountryList

cases.doc: The maximum number of country entries has been reached.
cases.name: MaxCountryEntriesReached

cases.doc: Account has been recovered and cannot be used.
cases.name: AccountRecovered

cases.doc: Metadata has too many entries (exceeds MAX_METADATA_ENTRIES).
cases.name: MetadataTooManyEntries

cases.doc: Metadata string value is too long (exceeds MAX_METADATA_STRING_LEN).
cases.name: MetadataStringTooLong

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when an identity is recovered for a new account.

udt_union_v0.name: CountryRelation

tuple_v0.doc: Country of citizenship

udt_union_v0.name: OrganizationCountryRelation

tuple_v0.doc: Country where funds originate

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: Error codes for the Token Binder system.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: TokenBinderError

cases.doc: The specified token was not found in the bound tokens list.
cases.name: TokenNotFound

cases.doc: Attempted to bind a token that is already bound.
cases.name: TokenAlreadyBound

cases.doc: Total token capacity (MAX_TOKENS) has been reached.
cases.name: MaxTokensReached

cases.doc: Batch bind size exceeded.
cases.name: BindBatchTooLarge

cases.doc: The batch contains duplicates.
cases.name: BindBatchDuplicates

udt_union_v0.doc:
Storage keys for the token binder system.

- Tokens are stored in buckets of 100 addresses each
- Each bucket is a `Vec<Address>` stored under its bucket index
- Total count is tracked separately
- When a token is unbound, the last token is moved to fill the gap
(swap-remove pattern)
udt_union_v0.name: TokenBinderStorageKey

tuple_v0.doc: Maps bucket index to a vector of token addresses (max 100 per bucket)
tuple_v0.name: TokenBucket

udt_union_v0.doc: Storage keys for the data associated with `RWA` token
udt_union_v0.name: RWAStorageKey

tuple_v0.doc: Frozen status of an address (true = frozen, false = not frozen)
tuple_v0.name: AddressFrozen

tuple_v0.doc: Amount of tokens frozen for a specific address
tuple_v0.name: FrozenTokens

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when underlying assets are deposited into the vault.
event_v0.name: Deposit

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when shares are exchanged back for underlying assets.
event_v0.name: Withdraw

cases.doc: Indicates access to uninitialized vault asset address.
cases.name: VaultAssetAddressNotSet

cases.doc: Indicates that vault asset address is already set.
cases.name: VaultAssetAddressAlreadySet

cases.doc: Indicates that vault virtual decimals offset is already set.
cases.name: VaultVirtualDecimalsOffsetAlreadySet

cases.doc: Indicates the amount is not a valid vault assets value.
cases.name: VaultInvalidAssetsAmount

cases.doc: Indicates the amount is not a valid vault shares value.
cases.name: VaultInvalidSharesAmount

cases.doc: Attempted to deposit more assets than the max amount for address.
cases.name: VaultExceededMaxDeposit

cases.doc: Attempted to mint more shares than the max amount for address.
cases.name: VaultExceededMaxMint

cases.doc: Attempted to withdraw more assets than the max amount for address.
cases.name: VaultExceededMaxWithdraw

cases.doc: Attempted to redeem more shares than the max amount for address.
cases.name: VaultExceededMaxRedeem

cases.doc: Maximum number of decimals offset exceeded
cases.name: VaultMaxDecimalsOffsetExceeded

cases.doc: Indicates overflow due to mathematical operations
cases.name: MathOverflow

udt_union_v0.name: VaultStorageKey

void_v0.doc: Stores the virtual decimals offset of the vault

event_v0.name: Approve

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when tokens are transferred between addresses.
event_v0.name: Transfer

cases.doc: Indicates an error related to the current balance of account from which\ntokens are expected to be transferred.
cases.name: InsufficientBalance

cases.doc: Indicates a failure with the allowance mechanism when a given spender\ndoesn't have enough allowance.
cases.name: InsufficientAllowance

cases.doc: Indicates an invalid value for `live_until_ledger` when setting an\nallowance.
cases.name: InvalidLiveUntilLedger

cases.doc: Indicates an error when an input that must be >= 0
cases.name: LessThanZero

cases.doc: Indicates overflow when adding two values
cases.name: MathOverflow

cases.doc: Indicates access to uninitialized metadata
cases.name: UnsetMetadata

cases.doc: Indicates that the operation would have caused `total_supply` to exceed\nthe `cap`.
cases.name: ExceededCap

cases.doc: Indicates the supplied `cap` is not a valid cap value.
cases.name: InvalidCap

cases.doc: Indicates the Cap was not set.
cases.name: CapNotSet

cases.doc: Indicates the SAC address was not set.
cases.name: SACNotSet

cases.doc: Indicates a SAC address different than expected.
cases.name: SACAddressMismatch

cases.doc: Indicates a missing function parameter in the SAC contract context.
cases.name: SACMissingFnParam

cases.doc: Indicates an invalid function parameter in the SAC contract context.
cases.name: SACInvalidFnParam

cases.doc: The user is not allowed to perform this operation
cases.name: UserNotAllowed

cases.doc: The user is blocked and cannot perform this operation
cases.name: UserBlocked
